data.title: Activist hedge fund ValueAct boosts stakes in Amazon, Meta in Q1 moves
data.description:
Activist hedge fund ValueAct Holdings boosted its stakes in Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META), among its first quarter moves.

ValueAct disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing, which was published on Thursday.

During the quarter, the fund increased its position on Amazon to 0.56 million from 0.54 million, while it piled class A shares of Meta to 1.01 million from 0.21 million. Other than that, ValueAct also raised its stakes in Visa (V).

According to the filing, the fund took a new stake of 6.73 million class A shares of Rocket Companies (RKT) and 3.42 million class A shares of Nu Holdings (NU).

Meanwhile, it reduced its position in Roblox (RBLX), Toast (TOST), Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) and Expedia Group (EXPE).

data.title: Gold rises as tame economic data boosts chance for Fed rate cuts
data.description:
Gold futures gained Thursday after U.S. economic data appeared to support the case for the Federal Reserve to continue cutting interest rates this year.

The surprise pullback in the U.S.-China trade conflict sparked a risk-on rally in the market to start the week, sapping gold's safe-haven appeal and driving investors to take profits, but focus on U.S. inflation data showed U.S. producer prices unexpectedly fell in April by the most in five years, while retail sales growth slowed, after a report earlier this week showed consumer prices rose less than expected in April.

Analysts said the data creates more room for the Fed to cut rates, with more dovish expectations building in the market; lower rates help increase the attractiveness of bullion, which is a non-interest bearing asset.

Russian President Putin's failure to show up for peace talks in Turkey dims expectations of progress towards a Ukraine peace deal, which may have helped to underpin gold prices.

The gold market appeared to ignore President Trump's comments that the U.S. was closer to an agreement that would curb Iran's nuclear program.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for May delivery closed +1.2% to $3,220.70/oz, its largest one-day dollar and percentage gain since May 6, and front-month May silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended +0.8% to $32.481/oz; YTD, gold has gained 22.5% and silver has climbed 12.2%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Michael Burry's Scion lightens up on healthcare portfolio, in Q1 moves
data.description:
Scion Asset Management, founded by Michael Burry of "Big Short" fame, exited five stakes in healthcare-related companies in Q1 2025, among other moves, according to the firm's 13F filing.

The firm closed out positions in American Coastal Insurance (ACIC), Bruker (BRKR), HCA Healthcare (HCA), Magnera (MAGN), Molina Healthcare (MOH), and Oscar Healthcare (OSCER), as well as apparel makers Canada Goose (GOOS) and VF Corp. (VFC).

It increased its stake in Estee Lauder (EL) to 200K shares ($13.2M) from 100K shares, according to the filing.

data.title: Troilus secures $35M loan facility to advance pre-development activities
data.description:
• Troilus Gold (TSX:TLG:CA) has secured a loan agreement of up to $35M with Auramet International.
  • This non-dilutive facility will support Troilus’ near-term development activities, including final permitting, early works preparation, and advancement toward full project financing.
  • An initial tranche of $15M was advanced to Troilus upon execution of the loan agreement. The remaining $20M will be available for drawdown 90 days from the date of the initial advance and subject to the satisfaction by Troilus of certain conditions precedent.
  • The loan bears 1.0% monthly interest on the outstanding balance, including principal and accrued interest, and is secured by a negative pledge on the company’s assets, a security interest and a hypothec charging personal property of the company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OFS Credit Company lowers April NAV per share estimate range
data.description:
Collateralized loan obligation investment company OFS Credit Company (NASDAQ:OCCI) announced on Thursday a lower range of its unaudited NAV per share estimate for April.

The estimate is between $6.12 and $6.22 as of April 30. Comparatively, the March estimate stood at $6.57-$6.67.

The unaudited range of NII per share estimate for the quarter ended April 30 is between $0.19 and $0.23. As of April 30, the company had $115.9M of term preferred stock outstanding.

data.title: Carl Icahn boosts stakes in CVR Energy, JetBlue, Illumina, among Q1 trades
data.description:
Carl Icahn, once known for his aggressive activism, boosted his stakes in CVR Energy (CVI), JetBlue (JBLU), Illumina (ILMN), and CVR Partners (UAN), according to his latest 13F filing.

Icahn increased his stakes in: CVR Energy (CVI) to 68.5M shares with a value of $1.33B at March 31, 2025, from about 66.7M shares; JetBlue (JBLU) to 33.6M shares ($162.1M) from 17.7M; Illumina (ILMN) to 220K shares ($17.5M) from 40K shares; and CVR Partners (UAN) to ~4.10M shares ($308.1M) from ~564.4M shares.

Meanwhile, the investor reduced his holdings in Southwest Gas (SWX) to 7.53M shares ($540.8M) from 9.63M in the prior quarter, according to the filings.

data.title: Paulson & Co takes new stake in Honeywell in Q1
data.description:
Paulson & Co, the hedge fund founded by John Paulson, takes a new stake in diversified industrial giant Honeywell International (NASDAQ:HON) in the first quarter, according to its latest 13F filing submitted on Thursday.

During the quarter, the fund boosted its stakes in Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (MDGL) to 2.1 million shares from 2 million, while it raised its position to 70.24 million shares from 64.2 million shares in International Tower Hill Mines (THM).

Apart from the new 200K shares in Honeywell, the fund also took a new stake of 21.5K shares in biopharmaceutical company Intra-Cellular Therapies (ITCI).

Meanwhile, Paulson closed out its position in Alphabet Class A shares (GOOGL).
